ops.text: Cross-repo (getaway plugin, ~/Code/getaway): the `getaway.onboard:nudge_d78e5911` inline hook test has two cases that fail nondeterministically under `capt-hook test` — Input(getaway:getaway "SFO to Tokyo…on points") and Input(getaway:refresh "refresh my balances") both "Expected Warn, got None". They carry no `llm={'fire': ...}` stub, so they invoke the real LLM judge and depend on it firing. Two distinct problems: (1) per the capt-hook judge-prompt-validation convention, inline nudge tests should stub the verdict (test the wiring, not the judge) — neighboring stubbed cases pass; (2) more importantly, the judge returned None (no warn) on *realistic* onboarding-relevant inputs, which suggests the onboard nudge prompt may be genuinely under-firing, not merely untested — stubbing the tests green would mask that. Surfaced while shipping the cc-skills codex-dispatch rework: it makes `plugins/repo-bootstrap/tests/test_verify.py` (which runs a fleet-wide `capt-hook test`) flaky, so a full local `uv run --project plugins/repo-bootstrap pytest` shows 5 failures unrelated to codex. Not in cc-skills CI (guides-lint runs only test_guides_fragments.py). Owner decision needed: strengthen the onboard nudge prompt vs. stub the two cases.
